message:
feat(tools): consult mailbox verdict to swap suspect live-db reads to archive snapshot

The tools crate is where MCP tool/resource reads actually hit the
database. Hook the new `mcp_agent_mail_db` verdict into every read
surface so a suspect live db transparently falls back to the
filesystem archive snapshot, instead of returning partial or
malformed results to callers that have no way to tell the underlying
db is wedged.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/resources.rs

- `resource_live_db_is_suspect(verdict)` centralizes the
  "is the live db suspect for resource reads" decision. Returns
  true when the verdict prefers archive snapshot reads for the
  primary read surface, AND the archive has enough metadata to
  answer the resource query. Consumed by every read resource
  handler in this file (project list, project detail, inbox,
  message, thread, file reservations, identity, capabilities,
  recent-tool-use, etc.). When true, the handler skips the live db
  entirely and serves from the archive snapshot reader; when false,
  the handler uses the existing live-db path.
- New branches in the resource dispatch handlers for the
  archive-fallback path. Each read resource now has three code
  paths: live-db, archive-snapshot (when verdict suspects live db),
  and not-available (when neither surface can answer the query).
- Error messages now distinguish "project not found" from
  "project found but backed by a suspect db" so callers can
  decide whether to retry after the reconstruction step runs.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/identity.rs

Identity lookups (`resource://identity/{path}`, the matching tool
calls, the capabilities resolver) now honor the verdict. When the
live db is suspect, identity resolution walks the archive's per-
project identity metadata instead. This keeps worktree-identity
features working through a partial db corruption window.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs

Message send / fetch / acknowledge paths gain read-side verdict
checks for the fetch surfaces (send still goes to the live db
since archive writes are not allowed). Fetch-inbox, fetch-thread,
and acknowledge-lookup now return archive-backed messages when the
verdict prefers archive snapshot reads.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/products.rs

Product-wide search, inbox, and thread summarization gain the
same verdict-driven fallback. When the verdict prefers archive
reads for any project linked to the product, the query composes
results from the archive snapshot for those projects and from the
live db for the rest, and the per-project verdict is surfaced in
the response metadata so callers can see which projects were
served from the archive vs. the live db.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/reservations.rs

File reservation listing (reads) now honors the verdict; create /
release (writes) still go to the live db. Documents the asymmetry
at the top of the reservation handler so future readers do not
wonder why the same handler mixes the two code paths.

## crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/lib.rs

Threads the verdict context through the tools crate's public entry
points, adds the re-exports for the new helpers, and plumbs the
archive snapshot reader into the shared state object the handlers
consume.
